questions.text: Wastewater released by houses, industries, hospitals, and offices is called:
questions.explanation: Sewage is liquid waste which causes water and soil pollution if not treated properly.

questions.text: Sewage is a complex mixture containing:
options: Organic and inorganic impurities, and nutrients
questions.explanation: Sewage contains various contaminants including bacteria, organic waste like urea, and inorganic salts.

questions.text: Which of the following is an organic impurity in sewage?
questions.explanation: Urea, animal waste, and oil are organic impurities, while nitrates and phosphates are inorganic.

questions.text: The full form of WWTP is:
questions.explanation: WWTP is the facility where wastewater is treated to remove physical, chemical, and biological contaminants.

questions.text: Which part of the WWTP removes large objects like rags, sticks, and cans?
questions.explanation: Wastewater is first passed through bar screens to filter out large physical debris.

questions.text: In the grit and sand removal tank, the speed of incoming wastewater is:
questions.explanation: The speed is decreased to allow sand, grit, and pebbles to settle at the bottom.

questions.text: The solids that settle at the bottom during the primary treatment of wastewater are called:
questions.explanation: Sludge is the solid waste removed from the bottom of the clarifier tank.

questions.text: Which device is used to remove floatable solids like oil and grease from wastewater?
questions.explanation: A skimmer removes floating impurities, while a scraper is used to remove sludge from the bottom.

questions.text: Water after the removal of sludge and scum is called:
questions.explanation: Clarified water is the intermediate stage where physical solids have been removed.

questions.text: Sludge is transferred to a separate tank where it is decomposed by:
questions.explanation: Anaerobic bacteria decompose sludge in the absence of oxygen, producing biogas.

questions.text: Biogas produced during sludge decomposition can be used as:
questions.explanation: Biogas is a renewable energy source consisting primarily of methane.

questions.text: Air is pumped into clarified water to help ________ bacteria to grow.
questions.explanation: Aerobic bacteria consume organic waste, food remains, and other unwanted matter in clarified water.

questions.text: The bacteria that settle at the bottom of the tank after aeration form:
questions.explanation: Activated sludge is about 97% water and contains active aerobic microbes.

questions.text: Which of the following chemicals is used to disinfect water?
questions.explanation: Chlorine and Ozone are commonly used to kill harmful microbes before water is released into distribution systems.

questions.text: Why should oils and fats not be released in the drain?
questions.explanation: Oils and fats can harden and clog sewage pipes; in open drains, they clog soil pores, reducing water filtration.

questions.text: Which of these should not be thrown into the sink as it might kill microbes that help treat water?
questions.explanation: Chemicals like paints, solvents, and medicines can kill the beneficial bacteria in the treatment process.

questions.text: Dried sludge is used as:
questions.explanation: Dried sludge returns organic matter and nutrients back to the soil.

questions.text: Which trees are suggested to be planted along sewage ponds to absorb surplus wastewater?
questions.explanation: Eucalyptus trees absorb surplus wastewater rapidly and release pure water vapor into the atmosphere.

questions.text: Poor sanitation and contaminated drinking water is the cause of a large number of:
questions.explanation: Lack of sanitation leads to water-borne diseases like cholera, typhoid, and hepatitis.

questions.text: Which of the following is a water-borne disease?
questions.explanation: Cholera is caused by ingesting contaminated water or food.

questions.text: An alternative arrangement for sewage disposal in areas where there is no sewerage system is:
questions.explanation: Septic tanks, chemical toilets, and composting pits are used for on-site sewage disposal.

questions.text: A toilet in which human excreta is treated by earthworms is called:
questions.explanation: Vermi-composting toilets convert human waste into nutrient-rich cakes using earthworms.

questions.text: The period 2005-2015 was declared as the International Decade for action on:
questions.explanation: The UN set a goal to reduce by half the number of people without access to safe drinking water.

questions.text: Which gas is used in the disinfection stage of water treatment?
questions.explanation: Ozone (O3) is a powerful oxidizing agent used for disinfecting water.

questions.text: The solid waste extracted from the sewage during treatment is:
questions.explanation: Sludge contains organic solids that are removed by a scraper.

questions.text: The process of removing contaminants from wastewater is called:
questions.explanation: Sanitation involves the collection and treatment of waste to maintain hygiene.

questions.text: One of the inorganic impurities present in sewage is:
questions.explanation: Phosphates and nitrates are examples of inorganic salts found in sewage.

questions.text: Meningitis and Polio are diseases that can be spread by:
questions.explanation: Many viral and bacterial diseases are transmitted through the fecal-oral route via water.

questions.text: What does 'Active Sludge' consist of?
questions.explanation: It contains a high concentration of microorganisms that digest organic matter.

questions.text: Which of these is a byproduct of wastewater treatment?
questions.explanation: Biogas and sludge are the two main useful byproducts of the treatment process.

questions.text: Chlorination is a ________ process of water treatment.
questions.explanation: It involves adding a chemical (chlorine) to destroy microbes.

questions.text: World Water Day is celebrated on:
questions.explanation: March 22 is designated to raise awareness about the importance of freshwater.

questions.text: The network of small and big pipes for carrying sewage is called:
questions.explanation: A sewerage system is an underground network that transports sewage to treatment plants.

questions.text: Septic tanks are suitable for places where:
questions.explanation: They provide on-site treatment for hospitals or isolated buildings.

questions.text: Human excreta is a health hazard if left in the open because:
options: It attracts flies and spreads diseases
questions.explanation: Open defecation leads to the contamination of water sources and soil, spreading epidemics.

questions.text: Which organism helps in vermi-processing toilets?
questions.explanation: Earthworms efficiently process human waste into high-quality compost.

questions.text: Which stage of WWTP uses a clarifier?
questions.explanation: Primary treatment involves physical separation of solids in a clarifier or settling tank.

questions.text: Scum is removed from wastewater by using a:
questions.explanation: A skimmer is designed to skim off floating oils and fats (scum).

questions.text: Un-treated human excreta can cause:
questions.explanation: It pollutes both surface water and groundwater.

questions.text: A good citizen should ________ to maintain sanitation.
questions.explanation: Individual responsibility is key to maintaining a clean and healthy environment.
